content.problemStatement:
You are given a sequence of non-negative integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã. A pair (x,y)(x, y)(x,y), where 1‚â§x<y‚â§N1 \le x \lt y \le N1‚â§x<y‚â§N, is an inversion if Ax>AyA_x \gt A_yAx‚Äã>Ay‚Äã.

You should answer QQQ queries (numbered 111 through QQQ). For each valid iii, in the iii-th query:


You are given a non-negative integer KiK_iKi‚Äã.
Consider a sequence B1,B2,‚Ä¶,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1‚Äã,B2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,BN‚Äã, where Bj=Aj‚äïKB_j = A_j \oplus KBj‚Äã=Aj‚Äã‚äïK for each valid jjj.
You should find the number of inversions in the sequence BBB.
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
the sum of QQQ over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10510^5105
the sum of QQQ over all test cases does not exceed 555
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and QQQ.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã.
each of the next QQQ lines describes a query with a single integer KiK_iKi‚Äã.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print QQQ lines. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines should contain a single integer ‚Äï the answer to the iii-th query.
